app.name: FitFix
app.tagline: FIT activity toolkit, in your browser

trust.local: 100% local — files never leave your device

home.headline: Fix, merge & convert any FIT activity — in your browser.
home.subhead: Drop a .fit or .tcx file from your watch or bike computer. The advisor finds what's wrong and offers a one-click fix. Your file never leaves your device.
home.drop: Drop one or more .fit or .tcx files here
home.two_files_hint: Add 1 file to open the unified Editor (advisor + tools + export). Add 2+ .fit files to use the legacy merge tool.

history.title: Recent activities
history.empty: Nothing here yet. Files you load are kept locally so you can resume them.
history.sample_error: Couldn't load the sample. Please try again.

merge.title: Merge split activities
merge.desc: Stitch 2+ .fit files into one continuous activity.
merge.requires: Add at least 2 .fit files to enable.
merge.bullet1: Watch ran out of battery mid-ride? Restart created two files? Connect refuses to combine them?
merge.bullet2: Distance, elevation, heart rate, laps, calories — all preserved and combined.
merge.bullet3: Drag to reorder. Output uploads cleanly to Garmin Connect, Strava, anywhere.

clean.title: Remove GPS jitter
clean.desc: Find spots where the watch sat still but GPS kept wandering.
clean.bullet1: Detects every stop where you didn't move but GPS drifted in spaghetti loops.
clean.bullet2: Pick a fix per cluster (single point / smooth line / leave alone) — see the impact instantly.
clean.bullet3: Recomputes lap and session distances so totals stop lying.

gpx.title: Convert FIT → GPX
gpx.desc: Plain GPX 1.1 export with HR, cadence, altitude, temperature.
gpx.bullet1: Use FIT files in apps that only read GPX (Komoot, RideWithGPS, Locus, Mapy.cz, OsmAnd…).
gpx.bullet2: Heart rate, cadence, altitude and temperature kept via Garmin TrackPointExtension v2.
gpx.bullet3: One trkseg per activity. Sport type set automatically from the FIT session.

editor.title: Open in Editor (preview)
editor.desc: All-in-one: see your activity, get suggested fixes, apply, export. Replaces the three tools below as features land.

capabilities.heading: What FitFix can fix
capabilities.sub: Drop one file. The advisor surfaces what's worth fixing — you stay in control.

merge.title: Merge split activities
merge.desc: Watch died mid-ride and you got two files. We stitch them.

elevation.title: Fix wrong elevation
elevation.desc: Garmin barometer says +200 m on a flat loop. We recompute.

trim.title: Trim forgotten start / end
trim.desc: You drove home with the watch on. We auto-detect and trim.

spikes.title: Remove HR / power spikes
spikes.desc: One bad sample ruined your VO2max. We replace it with the local median.

streams.title: Strip data streams
streams.desc: Junk HR, junk GPS on indoor, sensitive cadence — drop any stream.

indoor.title: Mark as indoor (Strip GPS)
indoor.desc: Strava flagged your Zwift ride as "in a vehicle"? One click.

privacy.title: Privacy zones
privacy.desc: Define a circle around home; we clip every point inside before export.

loops.title: Drop phantom GPS loops
loops.desc: Watch hallucinated 8 km of zigzag on a 100 km race? Yes, that happens.

timezone.title: Fix wrong timezone
timezone.desc: Activity uploaded at +9 h or in 1972? Shift it.

split.title: Split brick workouts
split.desc: Run + bike recorded as one file. Cut at any point, get two FITs.

view.title: See your data
view.desc: Multi-channel waveform per time — speed, elevation, HR, cadence, power, temperature.

convert.title: Convert FIT ↔ TCX, FIT → GPX
convert.desc: One unified import / export, no data loss for the channels TCX supports.

compare.heading: How FitFix compares
compare.lead: We're not trying to be GoldenCheetah. We're not trying to be Strava. Here's what we cover and what we send you elsewhere for.
compare.elsewhere_cheetah: Want full training analytics (FTP, TSS, power curves, PMC)? Use __LINK__ — open-source desktop, the gold standard.
compare.elsewhere_gotoes: Need just one cloud-based merge and you don't mind uploading? Use __LINK__ — the long-standing utility Strava itself recommends.
compare.elsewhere_fft: Want a one-off cloud edit for an obscure FIT field? Use __LINK__ — closed source, but covers the long tail.

editor.discard_confirm: You have unsaved edits. Leaving now discards them. Continue?

timeline.title: Values over time
timeline.hint: Hover/scrub the chart to read values · scroll to zoom · drag to pan

merge.panel_desc: Drop another .fit file to stitch onto this activity. The summary, timeline and map below preview the result before you apply.
merge.preview_explainer: Diffs in the summary and dashed lines on the timeline / map show what Apply would commit.

advisor.title: What we found
advisor.lead: Each finding opens the matching tool with details — review what would change before applying anything.
advisor.empty: No issues detected. The activity looks clean.

manual.title: More tools
manual.placeholder: No manual tools loaded yet. They register automatically as feature phases land.

tool.missing: Tool not available for this activity.

trim.desc: Cut leading or trailing dead time — drove home with the watch on, etc.

spikes.desc: One bad sample and HR jumps to 240 BPM, power to 2000 W. Replaces the outlier with the average of nearby samples.

elevation.desc: Recompute total ascent / descent or fix barometer drift.

privacy.desc: Define a circle around home / work; clip points inside before export.

timeshift.desc: Shift every timestamp (wrong timezone, sync error, year-of-1972 bug).

split.desc: Cut the activity at any point and download two FITs (brick run + bike, etc.).

strip.desc: Drop entire data streams: junk HR, junk GPS on indoor, sensitive cadence.

track.desc: Inspect / fix track type and FIT file metadata.

export.title: Export
export.fresh_id: Make file uploadable to Garmin Connect (new file ID)
export.fresh_id_help: Garmin Connect refuses files with an ID it has already seen. Bumping the ID lets you upload the result even if you previously uploaded the original.

slide_editor.alt: FitFix editor — map and advisor findings
slide_editor.caption: Editor: route on the map, advisor lists every fix worth a look.

slide_jitter.alt: GPS jitter sub-tool with per-cluster controls
slide_jitter.caption: GPS jitter tool: pin, smooth or keep — per cluster, with a live preview.

slide_clean.alt: Cluster fine-tuning view with map and detection settings
slide_clean.caption: Fine-tune each cluster on the map; numbers update as you decide.

how.title: How it works

step1.title: Drop
step1.body: Drop a .fit or .tcx from any device. Files load in your browser; nothing is uploaded.
step1.short: Loads in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.

step2.title: Review
step2.body: The editor opens with a map, channel summary, and an advisor panel. The advisor scans the file and suggests fixes — each card explains the problem in one sentence and offers one-click Apply or Dismiss. You can also open the manual tools drawer for full control.
step2.short: Advisor flags what's wrong; you click Apply or Dismiss.

step3.title: Export
step3.body: Download as .fit (bit-identical to your input except for the fields you changed), .gpx 1.1 with Garmin TrackPointExtension v2, or TCX 1.0. Re-upload to Garmin Connect, Strava, Komoot, anywhere.

how.screenshot_alt: Screenshot of the editor with the advisor panel
how.screenshot_caption: The advisor surfaces what's worth fixing — apply or dismiss each suggestion.

local.title: 100% local
local.body: Your .FIT files are processed entirely in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.

offline.title: Works offline
offline.body: Install as a PWA. Use it in the field, no internet required.

open.title: Open source
open.body: MIT-licensed on GitHub. Audit the code, fork it, run it yourself.

free.title: No accounts, no ads
free.body: No login. No tracking. No analytics. Free forever.

merge.title: Merging {{n}} files
merge.order_hint: Drag to reorder. Files are auto-sorted chronologically by default.

options.fresh_id: Make file uploadable to Garmin Connect (new file ID)
options.fresh_id_help: Garmin Connect refuses files with an ID it has already seen. Bumping the ID lets you upload the merged result even if you previously uploaded the originals.

clean.title: GPS jitter scan
clean.instruct: Pick a fix mode for each cluster. The savings update in real time.
clean.selected_summary: Will remove {{km}} km of fake distance ({{m}} m wandering)

options.title: Detection settings
options.fresh_id: Make file uploadable to Garmin Connect (new file ID)
options.fresh_id_help: Garmin Connect refuses files with an ID it has already seen. Bumping the ID lets you upload the cleaned result even if you previously uploaded the original.

modes.pin_help: Snap every point to one spot (the centroid). Best when you really didn't move — paused, fixing gear, waiting for a friend.
modes.smooth_help: Replace the wander with a triangle: start → farthest excursion → end. Best when you actually walked off somewhere and came back.
modes.keep_help: Don't change this cluster. Use when the GPS is actually right.

gpx.title: FIT → GPX
gpx.note: Heart rate, cadence, altitude and temperature are stored using Garmin TrackPointExtension v2 — Strava, Komoot, RideWithGPS and most other tools recognise it.

errors.not_fit: {{name}} doesn't look like a valid .FIT file.
errors.editor_needs_one: The editor opens one .fit file at a time.
